curation.synopsis: Cain and the 597th return to the frozen world of Nusquam Fundumentibus to break an Ork campaign that has already bled three Guard regiments before them. A daemon-possessed servitor wrecks their ship's bridge in transit; the controlled crash that follows hammers a wide crater into the permafrost and disturbs something far worse than Orks. Beneath the ice has lain a Tyranid hive ship dormant for some seven thousand years — older than Imperial presence on the world, older than the comet-strike folklore that supposedly formed the local geology. The remainder of the campaign becomes a three-way war with the slowly waking hive at its centre, and the closing chapters force Cain to confront the strategic implication: if Nusquam's hive predates first contact with the Tyranids by a millennium, the galaxy is far more infested than the Magos Biologis has ever admitted. The eighth Cain memoir.
